title: Canonical manifest (control plane)
description: datasets/manifest.json — the single control plane the frontend reads to discover every Parquet file, every taxonomy table, and every boundary geometry file. Per D21, the frontend NEVER guesses paths (R23); per ADR-0031, boundary discovery shifts to this manifest in Phase 0.8. Each table entry carries the Parquet/GeoJSON file inventory, partition columns (when partitioned), row counts, and the schema_version stamped into Parquet key-value metadata (which the reader fails-loud on if unsupported). Regenerated by the writer at the end of every emit (Phase 0.6).

x-changelog.description: Initial canonical manifest schema (Phase 0.3 of TODO/20260517-canonical-long-format-pivot.md). Carries D21 tables[] inventory: observation Parquet tables (with partition_columns + schema_version), taxonomy tables (compiled Parquet alongside the JSON source-of-truth), and boundary geometry files per ADR-0031 (geojson + pmtiles, format declared per-entry so the loader switches read paths data-driven). manifest_version is the writer's own version stamp; schema_version on each table is the table-shape version.

Add optional table_name (string) and kind (enum) fields to tables[].items. table_name is the explicit DuckDB-WASM view name, decoupling SQL identifiers from the on-disk Parquet filename per THE PLAN row 1.8a-bis (TODO/20260517-canonical-long-format-pivot.md) and canonical-store.md §2a. kind classifies the entry as observations | dim | taxonomy | other so admin/inventory does not have to string-match filenames. Both fields are optional in v1.1 for forward compatibility; the writer always populates both.

manifest_version.description: Writer's own manifest-shape version (separate from the table-level schema_versions). Bumps when the manifest's own shape changes.

generated_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC. When this manifest snapshot was emitted by the writer. NOT a per-row provenance field (those live on sources); this is single-shot operator telemetry for the control plane itself.

table_id.description: Dotted identifier <family>.<table> for canonical tables (e.g. 'elections.election_results', 'taxonomy.indicators') and <family>.<region>.<layer> for boundary layers (e.g. 'boundaries.in.states'). Stable; the frontend loader keys on this. Convention pinned in docs/architecture/data/canonical-store.md §12.2.

table_name.description: Explicit DuckDB-WASM view name for this table. Decouples the SQL identifier from the on-disk Parquet filename — frontend lib/duckdb.ts registerTable() prefers this over deriving from the table_id last segment, so the filename can evolve (e.g. observations.parquet -> election_results.parquet) without churning every view-model. Today equals the filename stem; PR-O onwards may differ. Optional in v1.1 for back-compat; writer always populates it.

kind.description: Coarse classification surfaced from the writer so consumers (e.g. backend/yen_gov/admin/inventory.py) need not string-match filenames. observations = long-format facts; dim = dim_*.parquet sibling; taxonomy = anything under datasets/taxonomy/; other = anything else recognised. Optional in v1.1.

format.description: Drives the loader read path. parquet -> DuckDB-WASM HTTP Range; geojson -> fetch + JSON.parse; pmtiles -> maplibre-gl native protocol; json -> taxonomy compiled-out JSON fallback (rare).

partition_columns.description: Hive partition columns when format=parquet AND table is partitioned (D8 partitions only when family > 15 MB; NEVER by year per R18). Empty array for unpartitioned tables. Typically ['indicator_id'] or ['topic_id'].

path.description: POSIX-relative path under datasets/ (e.g. 'elections/election_results.parquet', 'boundaries/in/geojson/india-states.geojson'). No leading slash; no .. segments.

size_bytes.description: File size; lets the loader budget HTTP Range buffer and report cold-load weight.

partition_values.description: Populated only when partition_columns is non-empty. Maps each partition column to this file's value (e.g. {indicator_id: 'state-installed-capacity-mw-coal'}). Lets DuckDB-WASM skip irrelevant files at query time.

row_count.description: Optional. Row count for Parquet tables; null for geometry files.

schema_version.description: Table-shape version. MUST equal the schema_version stamped into Parquet key-value metadata (writer enforces). Reader fails loud on unsupported version (D21).

row_count_total.description: Sum of row_count across files. Convenience denormalisation.
